summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. Backend: 18/18 new pytest cases pass for object-storage uploads (documents + safety), file list/download/soft-delete (cookie + ?auth= query token), My Approvals inbox, email-status, expiry-scan, invoice-reminders, and approval-action fire-and-forget email. Regression: 53/53 iter1+iter2 still green (total 71/71). Frontend: Documents drop-zone + click upload works (existing test.txt + safety items already in repo, new upload adds row); Safety per-row Photos dialog opens with image-only capture=environment input; topbar bell (my-approvals-btn) renders and dropdown opens (0 pending for admin after iter2 test runs consumed seeded approvals — backend confirms /api/approvals/inbox/mine includes newly created leaves); Clients/Vendors/Employees still show New + 5/4/6 edit/delete buttons under super_admin proving DataTableShell canWrite/canDelete default=false plus useResource() per-page passing works correctly. Server logs confirm 'Object storage initialised.' and 'email_enabled=True' at startup.
action_items: Optional: surface non-zero count badge on bell by creating a fresh pending approval — current admin inbox is empty because iter2 advanced all seeded chains to terminal state. Not a bug; UX consideration only.
Optional: add a periodic scheduler (e.g., APScheduler) to auto-run /api/notifications/expiry-scan + /api/notifications/invoice-reminders nightly. Endpoints currently only run on manual POST.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 942 lines — past the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers (files, notifications, approvals) in next iteration.

download_file in server.py has slightly convoluted auth logic: first calls get_current_user(request) inside a try/except, then re-validates the `auth` query param independently. Works but could be a single helper that returns the user from cookie OR query param. Edge case: if both are present and `auth` is invalid, it still raises 401 even though cookie was valid (returns early after `if auth:` branch). Low priority — covered by tests.

notifications.send_email already swallows exceptions; approval create/action wrap it in asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget which is correct and verified by the no-error test even when SDK fails for unverified recipients.

FileUploader.jsx download anchor uses cookie auth via same-origin <a> — works in browser but not from a fully cross-origin context. The ?auth= query-token path is implemented in the backend and verified, but not yet used in the UI. Consider switching the open link to include `?auth=${accessToken}` when same-origin cookies can't be relied on (e.g., embedded in iframes).
seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_* uploads in storage (1 .txt + 1 .png; the .png was soft-deleted in delete test, the .txt remains in /api/files for future regression). HR test user hr_it3_<runtag>@test.com left in users collection — safe to ignore.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter3 endpoints all green. POST /api/uploads requires multipart (file, folder, parent_type, parent_id, title). Folder must be 'documents' or 'safety' (400 otherwise). Download supports cookie OR ?auth=<access_token>. /api/approvals/inbox/mine super_admin returns all pending/in_progress with `_my_step` injected. /api/notifications/email-status returns {enabled:true}; expiry-scan + invoice-reminders restricted to super_admin/director/general_manager (+ accounts_executive for invoices) and return {scanned, sent, email_enabled}. Resend SDK silently no-ops on unverified addresses — sent=0 is correct, not an error. Topbar bell shows badge only when count>0; admin inbox is currently empty because iter2 ran the full approval workflows to terminal state.
